meaning: n.(暂时的)精神狂乱,精神错乱,说谵语状态,狂语,精神极度兴奋
examples: In her delirium, she had fallen to the floor several times.
Another newspaper article: DELIRIUM TREMENS TWAIN—Mark Twain, who was to make a speech at the mass meeting of the Independents last night, didn't come to time!
In my fever I had a long consistent delirium.
At times, her mind was in such hungry delirium that she imagined her pillow to be bread.
Crouch's flick was latched onto by Torres, who turned and shot in an instant to send the Kop into delirium.
